quotes.text: I do not understand artists who say they hate listening to their own records. I listen to mine obsessively. I'm looking for the error. There's always an error and it's always in a different place than where I thought it was.
quotes.speaker: Björk
quotes.context: Interview with The Wire magazine, published in their annual retrospective issue, December 2017
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Björk's interview language has this precise, slightly stern quality when she's discussing process, and compulsive self-scrutiny is completely authentic to her documented personality. The giveaway is the final clause, which is unusually neat for her idiosyncratic syntax.

quotes.text: My dad thought I was throwing my life away until the second time I went gold, and then suddenly he'd always believed in me. I have witnesses. My mother is one of them and she will confirm.
quotes.speaker: Bruno Mars
quotes.context: Acceptance speech remarks, backstage press room at the Grammy Awards, February 2018
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Mars is known for warm, quick-witted humor about his family and his come-up, and the 'I have witnesses' escalation sounds exactly like his comedic timing. The giveaway is how perfectly the punchline lands — real speeches are messier.

quotes.text: My grandmother used to say I was singing before I was talking, and honestly I believe her because I still find words a lot harder than notes. Like, ask me to explain something and I'll probably just start humming it at you.
quotes.speaker: Alicia Keys
quotes.context: NPR's Fresh Air with Terry Gross, aired February 2022, in conversation about her memoir 'More Myself'
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Keys frequently credits her grandmother and frames music as her primary language in interviews. The giveaway is that the humming punchline is a touch too tidy for genuine off-the-cuff speech.

quotes.text: I'm as excited about Reading & Leeds as I've ever been
quotes.speaker: Melvin Benn
quotes.context: NME interview with Reading & Leeds festival boss, 2026

quotes.text: There's a version of that record where the snare sits about three inches further back in the room, and I still think about it. Not with regret exactly, just — you know how you can hear a decision you made six years ago and it's like a splinter you forgot was there?
quotes.speaker: Trent Reznor
quotes.context: Interview with Sound on Sound magazine, October 2019 issue, discussing the production of 'With Teeth'
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Reznor genuinely obsesses over sonic minutiae and uses this kind of worn, self-interrogating language about his own work. The subtle giveaway is the 'three inches' spatial detail, which is just specific enough to feel engineered.

quotes.text: A rhinestone shines just as good as a diamond.
quotes.speaker: Dolly Parton
quotes.context: On being 'totally artificial' in appearance yet 'totally real' as an artist and person, in her November 2019 ELLE interview.
